predictions.text: A U.S. federal appeals court (or the Supreme Court) will issue an order staying (pausing) the implementation of the district-court order requiring the U.S. government to begin refunding $130B+ in tariffs, pending appeal.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if a published docket entry/order from a federal appellate court or the Supreme Court explicitly grants a stay (in whole or in material part) of the tariff-refund implementation order, pending appeal.
monitoring_signals: Docket updates in the relevant appeal (motions for stay pending appeal)
DOJ press statements or filings requesting emergency relief
CBP/US Treasury implementation notices being paused or revised
predictions.rationale: Refund orders of this scale typically trigger emergency stay requests; higher courts often pause implementation while merits are litigated.

predictions.text: Nvidia will disclose in an SEC filing (10-Q, 10-K, or 8-K) that newly proposed or newly implemented U.S. export controls on AI chips are expected to reduce revenue by at least $2.0 billion over one or more quarters (or equivalent quantified impact).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if an Nvidia SEC filing includes a quantitative statement (explicit dollar amount) indicating export controls will reduce revenue by >=$2.0B (or a substantially equivalent quantified sales impact) over a stated period.
monitoring_signals: BIS/Commerce export-control announcements and Federal Register notices
Nvidia earnings call transcripts and 10-Q risk-factor updates
Supplier/customer commentary on licensing delays or blocked shipments
predictions.rationale: If sweeping controls are pursued, Nvidia will likely quantify material impacts in risk disclosures and guidance updates.

predictions.text: Anthropic will file a lawsuit in U.S. federal court (e.g., D.D.C.) or the U.S. Court of Federal Claims challenging the Pentagon/DoD designation of Anthropic as a supply-chain risk (or seeking injunctive relief), and the complaint will be publicly accessible via court records.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if a complaint is filed and publicly viewable on PACER (or Court of Federal Claims docket) naming DoD (or a DoD component) and challenging the supply-chain risk designation, with Anthropic as plaintiff.
monitoring_signals: Anthropic public statements about litigation timelines
DoD procurement memos or contract actions referencing restrictions
Court dockets for Anthropic filings against DoD components
predictions.rationale: Anthropic signaled intent to challenge; litigation is a fast, concrete path to contesting procurement-related restrictions.

predictions.text: A U.S. GSIB (JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, or Wells Fargo) will publicly announce a tokenized U.S. Treasury/security issuance, repo, or settlement pilot with at least $1.0 billion of notional value executed/settled on a blockchain-based system (permissioned or public).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if the bank (or regulator) publicly states the transaction/pilot occurred and reports notional >=$1.0B involving tokenized Treasuries/securities or repo/settlement on a blockchain-based platform.
monitoring_signals: Bank press releases and investor presentations on tokenization
DTCC/FICC, Fedwire, or industry consortium announcements
Regulatory speeches referencing major tokenized Treasury pilots
predictions.rationale: Clearer capital treatment can accelerate large-bank pilots moving from small proofs-of-concept to meaningful notional sizes.

predictions.text: At least one of Moody’s, S&P, or Fitch will upgrade Wells Fargo’s long-term issuer credit rating by at least one notch (or upgrade the outlook from Negative to Stable/Positive) citing reduced regulatory overhang or remediation progress.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if Moody’s/S&P/Fitch publishes a rating action upgrading Wells Fargo’s long-term issuer rating by >=1 notch OR changes outlook from Negative to Stable/Positive, with rationale referencing regulatory remediation/enforcement action changes.
monitoring_signals: Rating-agency surveillance reports and outlook statements
Wells Fargo quarterly filings describing remaining consent orders
Regulator announcements ending additional enforcement actions
predictions.rationale: Closure of a major enforcement action often precedes rating actions once agencies gain confidence remediation is durable.

predictions.text: The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (FRED series DGS2) will close at or above 5.50% on at least one business day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if FRED DGS2 shows a value >= 5.50 on any business day on or before the deadline (using the published daily close).
monitoring_signals: Monthly CPI/PCE inflation surprises (especially energy pass-through)
Oil price spikes and inflation breakevens
Fed speaker tone and market-implied terminal rate
predictions.rationale: Sticky labor-cost dynamics plus an oil shock can push near-term policy expectations higher, lifting front-end yields.

predictions.text: OPEC+ will convene an extraordinary (out-of-schedule) ministerial meeting and announce a production-quota increase totaling at least 1.0 million barrels/day (aggregate), with an effective date no later than 2026-08-31.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if OPEC (or OPEC+ JMMC/ministerial statements) announces an extraordinary meeting and a quota increase >=1.0 mb/d (aggregate) with stated effective date <= 2026-08-31.
monitoring_signals: OPEC Secretariat announcements and meeting schedules
Saudi/UAE statements about stabilizing markets
Brent/WTI levels and physical market indicators (spreads, inventories)
predictions.rationale: Sustained war-risk premia and supply disruption risks raise pressure for spare-capacity holders to signal stabilization.

predictions.text: Amazon (or AWS) will publicly announce customer credits, service credits, or other remediation costs totaling at least $50 million related to the March 2026 outage, either in an earnings release/call, SEC filing, or official post-incident statement.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if Amazon discloses outage-related credits/costs >=$50M in an SEC filing, earnings materials, or an official Amazon/AWS statement that quantifies the dollar amount.
monitoring_signals: AWS Service Health Dashboard postmortems
Amazon quarterly filings and earnings Q&A
Large customer statements about credits received
predictions.rationale: Large-scale outages sometimes lead to material credits; quantification is more likely if costs become non-trivial.

predictions.text: S&P CoreLogic Case-Shiller U.S. National Home Price Index will show year-over-year growth at or above +6.0% in at least one monthly release (as published).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if any published Case-Shiller National Index release reports YoY percent change >= +6.0% (not seasonally adjusted as reported by S&P CoreLogic).
monitoring_signals: Inventory and new listings (relisting rates) vs demand
Mortgage rate moves and purchase application volume
Regional price acceleration in FHFA/Case-Shiller metro data
predictions.rationale: Low inventory and high relisting can support price re-acceleration even if affordability remains strained.

predictions.text: FDA will grant at least one Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) for a drug/device specifically indicated for a rare pediatric epilepsy syndrome, with the designation publicly disclosed by the sponsor.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if a biopharma sponsor press release or SEC filing states FDA granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation for an indicated rare pediatric epilepsy syndrome (e.g., Dravet, Lennox-Gastaut, CDKL5, etc.) before the deadline.
monitoring_signals: Sponsor press releases on regulatory designations
Clinical trial readouts (Phase 2/3 efficacy signals)
FDA designation announcements referenced in earnings calls
predictions.rationale: Strong seizure-reduction results in pediatric populations often prompt sponsors to seek expedited FDA pathways, including BTD.
